scheme_name: Coaching Sahay for the Preparation of NEET, JEE, GUJCET Examination to both Socially and Educationally Backward Class Trainees/Students and Female Students and Economically Backward Class Students
page_title: Coaching Sahay for the Preparation of NEET, JEE, GUJCET Examination to both Socially and Educationally Backward Class Trainees/Students and Female Students and Economically Backward Class Students

details: The “Coaching Sahay for the Preparation of NEET, JEE, GUJCET Examination to both Socially and Educationally Backward Class Trainees/Students and Female Students and Economically Backward Class Students” scheme was launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. It aims to provide financial aid to underprivileged students for coaching support. Under this scheme, students from SEBC or Economically Backward Class categories receive coaching fee (whichever is lower) for one exam-NEET, JEE, or GUJCET.
benefits: ₹20,000/- or actual coaching fee paid, whichever is lower.
Amount is credited via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the student’s bank account.
eligibility: The applicant should be a student.
The applicant must be from the SEBC or Economically Backward Class category.
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must have passed the Secondary School Certificate (SSC/Class 10).
The applicant must be enrolled in or preparing for NEET, JEE, or GUJCET.
The applicant must have paid a coaching fee and be able to submit a valid receipt.
The applicant’s annual family income must not exceed ₹6,00,000/-.

online: The eligible applicant may visit the e-Samaj Kalyan Portal:
On the home page, under the tab ‘Citizen Login’, click on New User ‘
Enter your Full Name as per Aadhaar Card, Gender, Date of Birth, Aadhaar Card Number, Email ID, Caste etc. and then click on ‘Register’.
After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
Now, click on ‘User Profile’ to update your profile.
Fill in all the mandatory information and then click on ‘Update’.
After updating the profile successfully, select the scheme appearing on the home page; an application form will open.
Fill in all the mandatory details in the application form and upload all the relevant documents.
Now agree with the terms & conditions and click on ‘Save Application’.

Submit the application form and take out the print of the application for future reference. The applicant can note down the application number to track the application status by logging into the portal using their User ID and Password.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Students from Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) or Economically Backward Classes (EBC), residing in Gujarat.

faqs.question: What exams are covered?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers NEET, JEE, and GUJCET exam preparation.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance is given?
faqs.answer: Up to ₹20,000 or the actual fee paid for coaching, whichever is lower.

faqs.question: What educational qualification is required?
faqs.answer: The applicant must have passed Class 10 (SSC).

faqs.question: How is the benefit amount disbursed?
faqs.answer: The amount is transferred through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to the applicant’s bank account.

faqs.question: Can students outside Gujarat apply?
faqs.answer: No, only permanent residents of Gujarat are eligible.

faqs.question: Can I apply offline?
faqs.answer: No, the application must be submitted online

faqs.question: What documents are required?
faqs.answer: Aadhaar card, caste certificate, income certificate, SSC mark sheet, bank details, age proof, and fee receipt.

faqs.question: Is there any application fee?
faqs.answer: No, applying under this scheme is completely free.

faqs.question: Where can I get more information?
faqs.answer: Visit https://sje.gujarat.gov.in/ddcw or the eSamajKalyan portal https://esamajkalyan.gujarat.gov.in for details.
